title: Herb-drug interactions among commonly used psychoactive substances by healthcare students

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a methodological/epidemiological study abstract describing a survey protocol to assess prevalence of concomitant use of psychoactive substances and herbs among healthcare students. No specific herb-drug interactions are described, quantified, or analyzed. The abstract discusses the general potential for interactions but does not present any documented cases, mechanisms, or evidence of actual interactions.

title: Analysis on the Status Quo and Requirement of Medication in Gestational Women and Effect of Pharmaceutical Care

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a methodological study on pharmaceutical care and medication safety in pregnant women. It reports survey results on medication patterns, educational interventions, and knowledge improvement, but does not describe any specific herb-drug interactions, drug-drug interactions, or pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic mechanisms. While TCM and Chinese patent medicines are mentioned as comprising 52.38% of drugs used, no specific interaction between any herb/TCM product and conventional drug is detailed or analyzed.

title: Preliminary study of the effects of β-elemene on breast cancer stem cells in cell line MCF-7/ADM

extraction.extraction_notes: This is an in vitro cell biology study investigating the effects of β-elemene (a TCM compound) on breast cancer stem cells and drug resistance protein expression in MCF-7/ADM cell lines. While β-elemene is a plant-derived substance and the study involves BCRP and P-gp (drug transporters), this is not a herb-drug interaction study. The abstract does not describe an interaction between β-elemene and a conventional pharmaceutical drug. Rather, it examines β-elemene's direct effects on cancer cell properties and transporter expression levels in isolation. No co-administered drug or pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic interaction with a conventional medication is reported.

title: A study to determine the knowledge and level of awareness of medical undergraduates about herbal medicines and herb-drug

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a survey/questionnaire study assessing medical students' knowledge and awareness of herbal medicines and herb-drug interactions. No specific herb-drug interactions are described or studied. The abstract discusses the lack of knowledge about HDIs among medical students but does not present any actual interaction data, mechanisms, or case examples. This is a methodological/educational paper, not a primary HDI research study.

title: EFFECTIVNESS OF COMPLEMENTARY AND ALTERNATIVE THERAPY FOR HIV/AIDS -

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a narrative review discussing the general use of complementary and alternative therapies (TCM, acupuncture, Ayurveda, aromatherapy, homeopathy, massage, meditation, naturopathy, reflexology) in HIV/AIDS patients alongside antiretroviral therapy. The abstract mentions that 'safety risks and potential drug interactions are frequently ignored' but does not describe any specific herb-drug interactions, mechanisms, or quantitative data. No particular herbs are paired with specific drugs with documented interactions. This is a general overview/commentary paper rather than a study reporting specific HDI cases.

title: INHIBITORY EFFECT OF AQUEOUS ROOT EXTRACT OF DECALEPIS HAMILTONII ON rOAT1

interactions.evidence_quote: The results shows significant decrease in CL & k 10 , and significant elevation in t 1/2 , AUC 0- & V ss of PAH in test group compared to control group clearly indicates the cumulative inhibitory effect of phenolic acids present in the AREDH. Further, the co-administration of AREDH with any hOAT1 substrate, results in decrease in clearance and increase in the bioavailability and half-life of the substrate drugs.

extraction.extraction_notes: Study conducted in Albino rats using Para Amino Hippuric acid (PAH) as OAT1 substrate probe. No specific conventional drug named; interaction described with general class of OAT1 substrate drugs. Sample size not explicitly stated for animal study. Clinical significance rated moderate as mechanism is clear but clinical relevance depends on specific substrate drug and therapeutic window.

title: THE NEED FOR HERBOVIGILANCE

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a review/perspective article on herbovigilance and pharmacovigilance systems. It discusses herb-drug interactions conceptually and identifies gaps in HDI reporting and detection, but does not describe any specific herb-drug interaction cases with identifiable herbs, drugs, mechanisms, or quantitative data. The abstract focuses on methodological and systemic issues in pharmacovigilance rather than reporting actual HDI evidence.

title: A role for physicians in ethnopharmacology and drug discovery.

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a perspective/review article on the role of physicians in ethnopharmacology and drug discovery. It discusses the general importance of ethnopharmacological approaches and mentions clinical studies on herbs and their interaction with modern medicines as a potential role for physicians, but does not describe any specific herb-drug interactions, mechanisms, or quantitative data. No concrete HDI cases are presented.

title: Lack of pharmacokinetic interaction between St. John's wort and prednisone.

interactions.evidence_quote: Twenty-eight days of SJW treatment resulted in no significant alterations in the pharmacokinetic parameters for prednisone or prednisolone. Oral administration of prednisone resulted in prednisone mean +/- SD area under the curves (AUCs) of 115.89 +/- 39.52 microg x h/L prior to SJW treatment and 128.76 +/- 32.71 microg x h/L after 28 days of treatment.

Prednisolone mean AUCs were 714.19 +/- 153.29 microg x h/L before SJW administration and 700.74 +/- 89.68 microg x h/L after treatment. Concurrent administration of SJW had no significant effect on the single-dose pharmacokinetics of prednisone or metabolic prednisolone in male subjects.

extraction.extraction_notes: Study examined potential CYP3A4-mediated interaction between St. John's wort and corticosteroids. Despite SJW's known CYP3A4 induction capacity, no clinically significant pharmacokinetic changes were observed for either prednisone or its active metabolite prednisolone. Minor numerical changes in AUC (11.1% increase for prednisone, 1.9% decrease for prednisolone) were not statistically significant. This represents a negative interaction study where expected interaction did not materialize.

title: Tanshinone IIA increases the bystander effect of herpes simplex virus thymidine kinase/ganciclovir gene therapy via enha

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes a basic science study on tanshinone IIA (a TCM-derived compound) enhancing the bystander effect of HSV-tk/GCV gene therapy in melanoma cells through gap junction upregulation. While tanshinone IIA is a plant-derived substance, there is no herb-drug interaction described in the pharmacokinetic or pharmacodynamic sense (i.e., no alteration of drug absorption, metabolism, distribution, excretion, or therapeutic efficacy through conventional drug-herb interaction mechanisms). The study focuses on a synergistic enhancement of gene therapy efficacy through a cellular communication pathway, not a classical HDI. No conventional pharmaceutical drug interactions are reported.

title: Effects of berberine on the pharmacokinetics of losartan and its metabolite EXP3174 in rats and its mechanism.

interactions.evidence_quote: The C<sub>max</sub> (1.26 ± 0.37 versus 1.96 ± 0.45 mg/L) and the AUC<sub>(0-</sub><sub>t</sub><sub>)</sub> (8.25 ± 0.89 versus 12.70 ± 1.42 mg h/L) of losartan were significantly (p < 0.05) increased by BBR compared to the control. We infer that BBR might increase the plasma concentration of losartan and decrease the concentration of EXP3174 through inhibiting the activity of CYP3A4 or CYP2C9.

the C<sub>max</sub> (0.97 ± 0.15 versus 0.77 ± 0.06 mg/L) of EXP3174 was significantly decreased compared to the control (p < 0.05). However, the T<sub>max</sub> of EXP3174 was decreased significantly (p < 0.05) from 8.14 ± 0.36 to 3.33 ± 0.28 h.

extraction.extraction_notes: Study conducted in Sprague-Dawley rats. Berberine (BBR) pretreatment at 20 mg/kg affected losartan pharmacokinetics. Mechanism inferred as CYP3A4 or CYP2C9 inhibition based on metabolic stability data from rat liver microsomes. Sample size for animal study not explicitly stated.

title: The effect of Chinese herbs and its effective components on coronary heart disease through PPARs-PGC1α pathway.

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a mechanistic study of a TCM formula (DanQi pill) and its components in a rat model of coronary heart disease. While the abstract mentions that DQP 'suppressed expression of cytochrome P450,' this is a pharmacological effect on an enzyme in the disease model, not a drug-drug or herb-drug interaction. No conventional pharmaceutical drug is co-administered with the herb or formula to assess interaction. The positive control (clofibrate) is used as a comparison group, not as a co-administered agent. The study focuses on the intrinsic mechanism of action of the herbal components (Salvianolic acids and Panax notoginseng saponins) on lipid metabolism pathways (PPARs-PGC1α), not on how these herbs alter the pharmacokinetics or pharmacodynamics of another drug. Therefore, this does not meet the definition of a herb-drug interaction.

title: The Synergistic Effects of Astragalus mongholicus and Salvia miltiorrhiza on Coronary Heart Disease Identi

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes a network pharmacology and experimental study of two Chinese herbs (Astragalus mongholicus and Salvia miltiorrhiza) for coronary heart disease treatment in an animal model. No conventional pharmaceutical drug is mentioned, and no herb-drug interaction is described. The study focuses on herb-herb synergy and mechanism of action against disease, not on interaction with conventional medications. Therefore, this does not meet the definition of a herb-drug interaction.

title: Mechanistic insights into the amelioration effects of diabetic cardiomyopathy by berberine: an integrated systems pharma

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a systems pharmacology and experimental validation study of berberine's effects on diabetic cardiomyopathy. While berberine is a plant-derived alkaloid from Coptidis Rhizoma (TCM), the abstract describes its therapeutic mechanism in disease treatment, not an interaction with a conventional pharmaceutical drug. No herb-drug interaction is described or implied.

title: Hepatoprotective diterpenes from the nodes of Pinus massoniana

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a phytochemistry/natural products isolation study. The abstract describes isolation and structural characterization of diterpenes from Pinus massoniana and their in vitro hepatoprotective activity in HepG2 cells against oxidative damage. No herb-drug interaction with a conventional pharmaceutical drug is described. The comparison to bicyclol (a hepatoprotective drug) is only a positive control reference for activity assessment, not an interaction study.

title: Exploring the Compatibility Mechanism of Xiaojianzhong Decoction Intervening in Chronic Atrophic Gastritis Rats Based on

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes a TCM formula (Xiaojianzhong decoction) mechanism study using serum pharmacochemistry and metabolomics in a rat model of chronic atrophic gastritis. While it discusses herb-herb compatibility within the formula (Jun-Chen-Zuo drug relationships), it does not describe interactions between the TCM formula/herbs and conventional pharmaceutical drugs. The focus is on intra-formula component interactions and metabolic pathway modulation, not herb-drug interactions. No conventional drugs are mentioned as co-administered partners.

title: Quality of manufacturer provided information on safety and efficacy claims for dietary supplements for colonic health

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a methodological review and survey study evaluating the quality of manufacturer-provided safety and efficacy data for dietary supplements for colonic health. While the abstract mentions that a literature review identified 'potential adverse events and drug-supplement interactions,' no specific herb-drug interactions are explicitly described or characterized. The abstract does not provide details on particular herbs, drugs, mechanisms, or interaction outcomes. It is a meta-analysis of data availability rather than a primary report of HDI findings.

title: Determination and pharmacokinetic comparison of rhein in rats after oral dosed with Da-Cheng-Qi decoction and Xiao-Cheng

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a pharmacokinetic methodology study comparing rhein absorption from two TCM decoctions (DCQD vs XCQD) in rats. While it describes PK parameters for a marker compound (rhein) from herbal formulas, it does not report an interaction between an herb/herbal formula and a conventional drug. The study is purely comparative PK analysis of the same active compound across two different TCM preparations without a drug co-administration component. No herb-drug interaction is described.

title: Reversal of multidrug resistance by natural substances from plants

interactions.evidence_quote: Capsanthin and capsorubin, carotenoids isolated from paprika, were identified as potent P-gp inhibitors
Capsanthin and capsorubin, carotenoids isolated from paprika, were identified as potent P-gp inhibitors
lycopene, lutein, antheraxanthin and violaxanthin induced moderate effects
lycopene, lutein, antheraxanthin and violaxanthin induced moderate effects
lycopene, lutein, antheraxanthin and violaxanthin induced moderate effects
lycopene, lutein, antheraxanthin and violaxanthin induced moderate effects
Among flavonoids, effective modulators were rotenone, chrysin, phloretin and sakuranetin
Among flavonoids, effective modulators were rotenone, chrysin, phloretin and sakuranetin

Among flavonoids, effective modulators were rotenone, chrysin, phloretin and sakuranetin
Some chloroform extracts of Chinese herbs were also found to inhibit MDR efflux pumps

extraction.extraction_notes: Abstract describes in vitro studies of natural compounds as P-gp and MRP1 inhibitors to reverse multidrug resistance in cancer cells. No specific conventional drug names are mentioned; interactions are with anticancer chemotherapy class generally. Specific drug names, sample sizes, and quantitative PK parameters are not provided. Clinical significance is moderate to low due to in vitro nature and lack of clinical data.

title: Interaction of green tea polyphenol epigallocatechin-3-gallate with sunitinib: Potential risk of diminished sunitinib bi

interactions.evidence_quote: The AUC0-∞ and C max of plasma sunitinib were markedly reduced by co-administration of EGCG to rats. Our study firstly showed that EGCG interacted with sunitinib and reduced the bioavailability of sunitinib.

extraction.extraction_notes: Interaction mechanism involves physical precipitation/complex formation in GI tract rather than enzymatic metabolism. Quantitative AUC/Cmax changes not specified numerically in abstract. Clinical case observation mentioned but not quantified. Evidence includes both in vitro (NMR/MS analysis, precipitation studies) and in vivo animal (rat bioavailability study).

title: Effect of pomegranate juice on intestinal transport and pharmacokinetics of nitrendipine in rats

interactions.evidence_quote: There was significant increase in effective permeability, absorption rate constant and fraction of drug absorbed in the pretreated group when compared with the control group, probably due to inhibition of the P-glycoprotein- mediated efflux of the drug by PJ. In comparison with control, PJ treatment significantly increased the are under the concentration-time curve of oral nitrendipine. The peak plasm concentration of nitrendipine was also significantly increased by PJ.

Pomegranate juice (PJ) is known to be potent inhibitor of human cytochromes (CYP), particularly CYP2C9 and CYP3A4. These results suggest that PJ inhibits the intestinal metabolism of nitrendipine without inhibiting the hepatic metabolism in rats.

extraction.extraction_notes: Two related mechanisms identified: primary P-gp inhibition at intestinal level (confirmed by absorption kinetics), and CYP inhibition potential (stated in introduction but study shows intestinal rather than hepatic metabolism inhibition). Sample size not explicitly stated in abstract. AUC and Cmax increased but exact percentages not provided in abstract text.

title: Intestinal absorption of forsythoside A in in situ single-pass intestinal perfusion and in vitro Caco-2 cell models

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a mechanistic absorption study of forsythoside A (FTA) from Forsythiae fructus using in vitro and in vivo animal models. While the abstract describes transporter interactions (P-gp, MRP, OATP inhibitors and enhancers), these are experimental tools used to characterize FTA absorption mechanisms, not herb-drug interactions with conventional pharmaceuticals. The study does not report interactions between the herb and any conventional drug in a clinical or therapeutic context. The compounds mentioned (verapamil, cyclosporine, diclofenac, indomethacin, mannitol, sodium caprate, EDTA, MK571) are used as probe substrates or inhibitors to elucidate transporter involvement, not as co-administered drugs with therapeutic intent.

title: Evaluating the pharmacological mechanism of Chinese Medicine Si-Wu-Tang through multi-level data integration

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a bioinformatics/network pharmacology study analyzing the potential targets and mechanisms of Si-Wu-Tang (SWT) formula through microarray data integration and computational analysis. While the abstract mentions that SWT targets can overlap with FDA-approved and experimental drugs, no specific herb-drug interactions are described. The study does not report actual pharmacokinetic or pharmacodynamic interactions between SWT components and specific drugs, nor does it present experimental evidence of interaction. This is a methodological/computational paper without explicit HDI cases.

title: Plant Phenols as Antibiotic Boosters: In Vitro Interaction of Olive Leaf Phenols with Ampicillin

interactions.evidence_quote: Synergistic interaction was observed between ampicillin and hydroxytyrosol.
In combination with ampicillin, OLE, caffeic acid, verbascoside and oleuropein showed additive effects.
In combination with ampicillin, OLE, caffeic acid, verbascoside and oleuropein showed additive effects.
In combination with ampicillin, OLE, caffeic acid, verbascoside and oleuropein showed additive effects.

extraction.extraction_notes: In vitro study evaluating antimicrobial synergy between olive leaf phenols and ampicillin. Multiple phenolic compounds tested individually; no sample size specified for in vitro assays. Clinical significance rated as moderate for synergistic interaction and low for additive effects given in vitro nature and lack of human data.

title: Metabolic profiling of quercetin in rats using ultra-performance liquid chromatography/quadrupole-time-of-flight mass sp

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a pharmacokinetic metabolism study of quercetin in rats using UPLC-Q-TOF/MS. While quercetin is identified as a flavonoid from traditional Chinese medicines, the abstract focuses solely on metabolite profiling and does not describe any herb-drug interaction, co-administration with conventional drugs, or interaction mechanism with pharmaceutical agents. No drug partner is mentioned.

title: Initial dose optimization of tacrolimus for children with systemic lupus erythematosus based on the CYP3A5 polymorphism

interactions.evidence_quote: The results showed that weight, the CYP3A5 genotype and combined treatment with Wuzhi capsule can affect tacrolimus clearance in children with systemic lupus erythematosus. Furthermore, at the same weight, the ratios of tacrolimus clearance were 1:2.49:0.57:1.4193 from people who were CYP3A5*3/*3 and did not take Wuzhi capsule, people who had the CYP3A5*1 allele and did not take Wuzhi capsule, people who were CYP3A5*3/*3 and took Wuzhi capsule, and people who had the CYP3A5*1 allele and took Wuzhi capsule, respectively.

extraction.extraction_notes: Wuzhi capsule composition not explicitly detailed in abstract; herb species and active compounds not specified. Clearance reduction calculated from ratio 0.57 relative to baseline (1.0), representing approximately 43% reduction for CYP3A5*3/*3 genotype with Wuzhi. Study is population PK modeling in pediatric patients with SLE; sample size not explicitly stated. Interaction is genotype-dependent and clinically significant for dose optimization.

title: Exploring herb-drug interaction on glycemic effect in preclinical studies – a human safety perspective

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a narrative review article that discusses herb-drug interactions in general terms (pharmacodynamic and pharmacokinetic interactions with oral hypoglycemic drugs) but does not describe any specific herb-drug interaction cases with identified herbs, drugs, mechanisms, or quantitative data. The abstract provides methodological overview and general conclusions about the safety of concurrent herbal and allopathic use without detailing particular interactions.

title: Medicinal herbs with anti-depressant effects

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a narrative review article summarizing antidepressant effects of various medicinal herbs (St. John's Wort, saffron, turmeric, ginkgo, chamomile, valerian, lavender, Echium amoenum, Rhodiola rosea). While the abstract mentions that conventional drugs may cause drug-drug or drug-food interactions, no specific herb-drug interactions are described or quantified. The paper focuses on the efficacy and safety profile of herbs compared to synthetic drugs, not on mechanistic interactions between herbs and conventional pharmaceuticals.

title: Effects of Herba Erigerontis injection on pharmacodynamics and pharmacokinetics of warfarin in rats in vivo

interactions.evidence_quote: Low (3.6 mL/kg), medium (7.2 mL/kg) and high (10.8 mL/kg) doses of HEI could decrease plasma protein binding rate of warfarin significantly (P <.01). The results mean that HEI can potentiate the anticoagulant response of warfarin through both pharmacodynamics and pharmacokinetics.
Pharmacokinetic study manifested that Cmax, AUC and t1/2 prolonged significantly (P <.01) for R/S-warfarin in presence of HEI.

extraction.extraction_notes: Two distinct interactions extracted: (1) protein binding displacement mechanism with pharmacodynamic potentiation of anticoagulant effect; (2) pharmacokinetic interaction with increased Cmax, AUC, and half-life. Sample size not explicitly stated in abstract. Specific active compounds of HEI not identified in abstract.

title: Network Pharmacology-Based Analysis of Xiao-Xu-Ming Decoction on the Treatment of Alzheimer’s Disease

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a network pharmacology and in silico study of a TCM formula (Xiao-Xu-Ming Decoction) for Alzheimer's disease treatment. The abstract describes target prediction, molecular docking, and in vitro cell-based assays of active constituents, but does not report any herb-drug interactions with conventional pharmaceuticals. The study focuses on the formula's potential therapeutic targets (COX-2, ERα, AChE) and lead compounds (fanchinoline, dauricine) rather than interactions between the formula/herbs and co-administered drugs. No pharmacokinetic or pharmacodynamic interactions with conventional drugs are described.

title: Exploration of components and mechanisms of Polygoni Multiflori Radix-induced hepatotoxicity using siRNA -mediated CYP3A

interactions.evidence_quote: PMR and EM significantly inhibited the mRNA expression of CYP3A4 and UGT1A1 in L02 cells
PMR and EM significantly inhibited the mRNA expression of CYP3A4 and UGT1A1 in L02 cells
TSG and GA activated the mRNA expression of CYP3A4 and UGT1A1
TSG and GA activated the mRNA expression of CYP3A4 and UGT1A1

extraction.extraction_notes: This study investigates hepatotoxicity of Polygoni Multiflori Radix (PMR) and its components through modulation of drug metabolism enzymes CYP3A4 and UGT1A1. While not a traditional herb-drug interaction study with a specific pharmaceutical drug, the abstract describes interactions between herbal components and critical drug-metabolizing enzymes that would affect drug metabolism. The interactions are characterized as pharmacokinetic through enzyme inhibition/induction mechanisms. Four main components were studied: TSG, emodin (EM), EG, and gallic acid (GA). EM and GA were identified as main hepatotoxic components. The study is in vitro using knockdown cell lines (L02 and HepaRG cells).

title: Systematic investigation on the anti-rheumatoid arthritis material basis and mechanism of Juan Bi Tang. Part 1: Integrat

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a mechanistic study of a TCM formula (Juan Bi Tang) using metabolic profiling and network pharmacology in animal models. It describes the absorption, metabolism, and putative anti-RA targets of JBT constituents, but does not report any herb-drug interactions with conventional pharmaceuticals. The study focuses on the formula's intrinsic mechanism of action against rheumatoid arthritis, not on interactions between JBT and co-administered drugs.

title: The influence of Javanese turmeric (Curcuma xanthorrhiza) on the pharmacokinetics of warfarin in rats with single and mu

interactions.evidence_quote: The area under the curve (AUC) of R- and S-WF in the CX-2 group was a significantly higher value compared to the control (77.54 vs. 35.27 mg.h/L for R-WF and 316.26 vs. 40.16 mg.h/L for S-WF; p < 0.05; Kruskal-Wallis method).
The CX-2 administration also caused the increasing in the concentration level of R-WF (16%) and S-WF (27%) from the 7th to 9th day of administration.

extraction.extraction_notes: Two separate interactions extracted: (1) single-dose study showing AUC increase for both R- and S-warfarin enantiomers at CX-2 (18 mg/kg) dose; (2) multiple-dose study showing concentration increase over days 7-9. Mechanism unspecified in abstract. Clinical significance rated high due to warfarin's narrow therapeutic index and significant PK changes (>50% AUC increase for S-warfarin). Study used rat model with n=6 per group.

title: Screening out anti-inflammatory or anti-viral targets in Xuanfei Baidu Tang through a new technique of reverse finding t

extraction.extraction_notes: This is an in silico computational study using reverse pharmacology to identify potential anti-inflammatory and anti-viral targets of Xuanfei Baidu Tang (XBT) for COVID-19 treatment. No specific herb-drug interactions are described. The study focuses on target prediction and mechanism of action of the TCM formula itself, not on interactions between herbs and conventional drugs. No quantitative pharmacokinetic or pharmacodynamic interaction data between XBT components and pharmaceutical drugs are presented.

title: Improvement of Astragalin on Spermatogenesis in Oligoasthenozoospermia Mouse Induced by Cyclophosphamide

interactions.evidence_quote: Astragalin also significantly reversed the reduction in body weight, reproductive organs index, and sperm parameters (sperm concentration, viability, and motility) induced by CTX, and restored testicular abnormal histopathologic morphology induced by CTX.

extraction.extraction_notes: This study describes a protective/antagonistic interaction where astragalin (a TCM-derived compound) mitigates the adverse effects of cyclophosphamide on spermatogenesis in mice. The interaction is pharmacodynamic in nature, with astragalin counteracting CTX-induced testicular damage and apoptosis. Sample size not explicitly stated for animal cohorts. Evidence is in vivo animal model only; clinical significance is moderate as it demonstrates proof-of-concept but requires human validation.

title: Characterization of diterpene metabolism in rats with ingestion of seed products from Euphorbia lathyris L. (Semen Eupho

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a pure metabolite characterization study of Euphorbia lathyris diterpenes in rats. No herb-drug interaction is described or implied. The study focuses on identifying metabolic pathways and metabolite products of plant diterpenoids in isolation, without any co-administration with conventional drugs or assessment of pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic interactions with pharmaceuticals.

title: Chemical profiling and identification of Radix Cudramiae and their metabolites in rats using an ultra-high-performance l

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a chemical profiling and metabolite identification study of Radix Cudramiae using UHPLC-MS/MS in rats. No herb-drug interactions are described. The abstract focuses on analytical methodology for characterizing natural compounds and their metabolites in the herb, not on interactions with conventional drugs or other herbs.

title: Network pharmacology-based anti-colorectal cancer activity of piperlonguminine in the ethanolic root extract of Piper lo

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a network pharmacology and in silico study focused on the anti-cancer potential of piperlonguminine from Piper longum L. The abstract describes computational target prediction, pathway analysis, and molecular docking approaches but does not report any actual herb-drug interactions with conventional pharmaceuticals. No pharmacokinetic or pharmacodynamic interaction data with drugs is presented. The study is purely predictive/mechanistic in nature without clinical or experimental validation of interactions.

title: Shenge Formula attenuates high-fat diet-induced obesity and fatty liver via inhibiting ACOX1

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a mechanistic study of Shenge Formula (SGF), a TCM preparation, in a mouse model of NAFLD. The abstract describes the pharmacological mechanism of SGF (ACOX1 inhibition, PPARα activation, lipid metabolism regulation) but does not describe any herb-drug interaction with a conventional pharmaceutical drug. Metformin is mentioned only as a positive control comparator, not as a co-administered drug with SGF. No drug-herb or herb-herb interaction is reported.

title: A new strategy of enhancing absorptivity and safety for evodiamine: Polyamidoamine derivatives modified by polyethylene

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes a pharmaceutical formulation study focused on improving the bioavailability of evodiamine (a TCM alkaloid) using PAMAM-co-PEG excipients. It is a methodology/drug delivery paper with no herb-drug interactions or interactions between evodiamine and conventional drugs described. The study examines solubility, permeability, and safety of evodiamine alone and with the novel excipient, but does not report any interactions with conventional pharmaceutical drugs.

title: Traditional Chinese medicines Wu Wei Zi (Schisandra chinensis Baill) and Gan Cao (Glycyrrhiza uralensis Fisch) activate

interactions.evidence_quote: In rats, the administration of Wu Wei Zi and Gan Cao increased the metabolism of the coadministered warfarin, reinforcing concerns involving the safe use of herbal medicines and other nutraceuticals to avoid PXR-mediated drug-drug interactions.

In rats, the administration of Wu Wei Zi and Gan Cao increased the metabolism of the coadministered warfarin, reinforcing concerns involving the safe use of herbal medicines and other nutraceuticals to avoid PXR-mediated drug-drug interactions.

extraction.extraction_notes: Two separate TCM herbs tested together in the same rat study. Both activate PXR and induce CYP3A and other drug-metabolizing enzymes, leading to increased warfarin clearance. Quantitative PK changes not provided in abstract. Active compounds identified for Wu Wei Zi (Schisandrol and Schisandrin) but not for Gan Cao.

title: Chinese herbal formula Qilong-Lishui granule improves puromycin aminonucleoside-induced renal injury through regulation

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a preclinical pharmacology study investigating the therapeutic mechanism of a Chinese herbal formula (Qilong-Lishui granule) in an animal model of kidney disease. While the abstract mentions a herbal formula and discusses molecular pathways, it does not describe any herb-drug interaction (HDI) with a conventional pharmaceutical drug. The study compares QLG treatment to fosinopril (an ACE inhibitor) as a positive control, but does not report any pharmacokinetic or pharmacodynamic interaction between QLG and fosinopril or any other drug. The focus is on the independent therapeutic efficacy of QLG through BMP signaling modulation, not on drug-herb interaction mechanisms.

title: Albumin's Influence towards Hepatic Metabolism and Metabolism-Related Drug-Drug Interaction with Aurantio-Obtusin

interactions.evidence_quote: BSA supplement significantly decreased the inhibition potential of aurantio-obtusin towards UGT1A1 and UGT1A3
BSA supplement significantly decreased the inhibition potential of aurantio-obtusin towards UGT1A1 and UGT1A3

extraction.extraction_notes: Aurantio-obtusin is a bioactive ingredient from Semen Cassiae (a TCM herb). Study is in vitro using human liver microsomes and recombinant UGT enzymes. No specific conventional drug partner is identified; the interaction potential is demonstrated with UGT isoforms. Clinical significance is low as this is mechanistic in vitro work without direct drug co-administration data. The study demonstrates aurantio-obtusin can inhibit UGT1A1 and UGT1A3, which could theoretically increase exposure of UGT-metabolized drugs, but no specific drug is tested.

title: Direct and indirect risk associated with the use of dietary supplements among persons with dementia in a Norwegian memor

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a survey/observational study describing the prevalence of dietary supplement use among persons with dementia and assessment of direct/indirect risks. While the abstract mentions that 'potentially clinically relevant DS-drug interactions' were found in 8 persons (11% of users), no specific herb-drug interactions are described or detailed. The study does not identify which dietary supplements, which drugs, or what specific interactions were observed. Therefore, this does not meet the threshold for contains_hdi=true, as no extractable HDI data is provided.

title: Potential Drug-Drug and Herb-Drug Interactions in Patients With Cancer: A Prospective Study of Medication Surveillance

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a prospective surveillance study that reports aggregate statistics on the prevalence of herb-drug interactions (HDIs) and drug-drug interactions (DDIs) in cancer patients, but does not describe specific herb-drug pairs, mechanisms, or quantitative interaction data. The abstract identifies that 84 patients used herbal supplements and 122 possible HDIs were detected, but no individual interactions are characterized with herb names, drug names, mechanisms, or direction of effect. This is a methodological/epidemiological study rather than a mechanistic or case-based HDI report.

title: Inhibition of Cytochrome P450 Activities by Extracts of Hyptis verticillata Jacq.: Assessment for Potential HERB-Drug In

interactions.evidence_quote: Individual screening of these phytochemicals against the activity of CYP1A2 identified yatein as a moderate inhibitor (71.9 mu M), likely to contribute to the plant extract's potent bioactivity.
The dried plant aqueous extract demonstrated potent inhibition of the activities of CYPs 1A1 (7.6 mu g/mL), 1A2 (1.9 mu g/mL), 1B1 (9.4 mu g/mL) and 3A4 (6.8 mu g/mL).
The dried plant aqueous extract demonstrated potent inhibition of the activities of CYPs 1A1 (7.6 mu g/mL), 1A2 (1.9 mu g/mL), 1B1 (9.4 mu g/mL) and 3A4 (6.8 mu g/mL).
The dried plant aqueous extract demonstrated potent inhibition of the activities of CYPs 1A1 (7.6 mu g/mL), 1A2 (1.9 mu g/mL), 1B1 (9.4 mu g/mL) and 3A4 (6.8 mu g/mL).
The dried plant aqueous extract demonstrated potent inhibition of the activities of CYPs 1A1 (7.6 mu g/mL), 1A2 (1.9 mu g/mL), 1B1 (9.4 mu g/mL) and 3A4 (6.8 mu g/mL).

extraction.extraction_notes: Abstract describes in vitro CYP inhibition by Hyptis verticillata extracts but does not specify particular drugs affected. Interactions are potential rather than demonstrated with specific pharmaceuticals. Multiple CYP isoforms inhibited; extracted as separate interactions for each target. No specific drug names mentioned, so drug_name and drug_class are null. Clinical significance rated as low-moderate due to in vitro nature and lack of clinical validation.

title: Effects of Baoyuan decoction, a traditional Chinese medicine formula, on the activities and mRNA expression of seven CYP

interactions.evidence_quote: BYD at low (1.46 g/kg) and high (7.30 g/kg) dosages could significantly increase C-max and AUC(0-t) of chlorzoxazone
BYD at low (1.46 g/kg) and high (7.30 g/kg) dosages could significantly increase C-max and AUC(0-t) of chlorzoxazone and testosterone
BYD at low (1.46 g/kg) and high (7.30 g/kg) dosages could significantly increase C-max and AUC(0-t) of chlorzoxazone and testosterone, while decrease AUC(0-t) of phenacetin at high dosage
BYD could significantly increase AUC(0-t) of tolbutamide and metoprolol
BYD could significantly increase AUC(0-t) of tolbutamide and metoprolol
BYD had increased AUC(0-t) of bupropion at low dosage and decreased it at high dosage

extraction.extraction_notes: Baoyuan decoction is a TCM formula composed of four herbs but specific herb names and active compounds are not provided in the abstract. The study evaluated effects on seven CYP isozymes in rats. Multiple probe drugs were used to assess CYP activity modulation. Direction marked as context_dependent for bupropion due to dose-dependent bidirectional effect.

title: Molecular and biochemical monitoring of the possible herb-drug interaction between Momordica charantia extract and glibe

interactions.evidence_quote: GLB plus MCFE combination was better than GLB alone in improving levels of insulin and HbA1c. Moreover, GLB plus MCFE-500 was the most efficient in restoring INR, Slc2a2 and PPAR-alpha mRNA expression to their normal levels.

extraction.extraction_notes: Study demonstrates synergistic pharmacodynamic interaction in diabetic rats. Momordica charantia fruit extract (MCFE) enhances glibenclamide's glycemic control and insulin secretion effects. Sample size not explicitly stated for each group. Mechanism appears to involve enhanced insulin receptor expression and glucose transporter upregulation rather than direct pharmacokinetic alteration.

title: A chromosome-scale genome of Peucedanum praeruptorum provide insights into Apioideae evolution and medicinal ingredient

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a genomics and plant biology paper focused on genome sequencing, assembly, and comparative analysis of Peucedanum praeruptorum. It describes gene families involved in coumarin and terpenoid biosynthesis but does not describe any herb-drug interactions, pharmacokinetic studies, or clinical/pharmacological interactions with conventional drugs. No HDI content present.

title: A Study on the Protective Impact of Resveratrol on Liver Damage in Rats with Obstructive Jaundice

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a network pharmacology and mechanistic study of resveratrol's protective effects in obstructive jaundice in rats. While the abstract mentions CYP enzymes (CYP2C9, CYP1A1, CYP3A4) as core targets and discusses xenobiotic metabolism pathways, it does not describe a specific herb-drug interaction between resveratrol and a conventional pharmaceutical drug. The study focuses on resveratrol's therapeutic mechanism in treating liver injury, not on how resveratrol alters the pharmacokinetics or pharmacodynamics of a co-administered drug. No drug-herb co-administration or interaction is reported.
